name: Suspicious Binary Load and Execution
notes: This rule triggers when an arbitrary dll is loaded or executed from a specified binary.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Execution DLL of Choice Using WAB
BB:BehaviorDefinition: Runs COM Object via Verclsid
BB:BehaviorDefinition: Specified Binary Execution via Devtoolslauncher or OpenWith
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: DLL Execution via Rasautou, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Execute Files with Msdeploy, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Execution via Diskshadow, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Execution via stordiag, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Execution DLL of Choice Using WAB, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Execution via WorkFolders, BB:BehaviorDefinition: File Download Using ProtocolHandler, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Specified Binary Execution via Devtoolslauncher or OpenWith, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Registration via cscript, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Runs COM Object via Verclsid, BB:BehaviorDefintion: Proxy Execution Via Explorer
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious Binary Load and Execution on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: DLL Execution via Rasautou, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Execute Files with Msdeploy, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Execution via Diskshadow, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Execution via stordiag, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Execution DLL of Choice Using WAB, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Execution via WorkFolders, BB:BehaviorDefinition: File Download Using ProtocolHandler, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Specified Binary Execution via Devtoolslauncher or OpenWith, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Registration via cscript, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Runs COM Object via Verclsid, BB:BehaviorDefintion: Proxy Execution Via Explorer
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious Binary Load and Execution
Event description: Detects when an arbitrary dll is loaded or executed from a specified binary.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
